message:
feat(sqlmodel): add table inheritance strategy support

Implements SQLAlchemy-style table inheritance patterns for derived models:

InheritanceStrategy enum with four modes:
- None (default): No inheritance relationship, standard table
- Single: All subclasses in one table with discriminator column
- Joined: Each class has own table with FK to parent (normalized)
- Concrete: Each concrete class is independent, no DB-level inheritance

New derive macro attributes:
- #[sqlmodel(inheritance = "single|joined|concrete")]: Set strategy on base
- #[sqlmodel(inherits = "ParentModel")]: Declare parent relationship
- #[sqlmodel(discriminator_value = "child_type")]: Polymorphic identity value

InheritanceInfo struct captures:
- strategy: The inheritance mode
- parent_type: Optional parent model type path
- discriminator_value: Optional polymorphic identity for single-table

Parse layer extracts inheritance metadata from attribute macros and
validates combinations (e.g., discriminator_value requires single-table
inheritance or explicit discriminator column on parent).

This enables ORM patterns like:
```rust
#[derive(SQLModel)]
#[sqlmodel(inheritance = "single", discriminator = "type")]
struct Employee { id: i32, name: String }

#[derive(SQLModel)]
#[sqlmodel(inherits = "Employee", discriminator_value = "manager")]
struct Manager { department: String }
```

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
